questions.question: What is the correct syntax for class B inheriting from class A?
questions.explanation: In Python, inheritance uses parentheses: class Child(Parent): . The other options are not valid Python syntax.

questions.question: What does super() do inside a child class method?
options: Calls a method from the parent class
questions.explanation: super() gives you access to the parent class's methods. It's commonly used in __init__ to run the parent's setup code before adding child-specific attributes.

questions.question: What is method overriding?
options: Deleting a method from the parent
Redefining a parent method in the child class
Creating two methods with the same name
Importing a method from another file
questions.explanation: Method overriding means writing a new version of a parent method in the child class. When you call the method on a child instance, the child's version runs instead of the parent's.

questions.question: What built-in function checks whether an object is an instance of a class? _____
If Dog inherits from Animal, what does isinstance(dog, Animal) return?
questions.explanation: isinstance returns True because Dog IS an Animal through inheritance. A child instance is considered an instance of the parent class too.

questions.question: What does polymorphism allow you to do?
options: Have multiple classes with the same method name behave differently
Have many methods in one class
Use multiple files in a project
questions.explanation: Polymorphism means different classes respond to the same method call in their own way. You can write code that works on a parent type and it automatically works for all child types.
